articles.headline: South Africa vs. South Korea - Kick-off time, how to watch, Bafana permutations, team news
articles.description: Hugo Broos' Bafana Bafana will almost certainly need a win when they face South Korea in Guadalupe in their last FIFA World Cup Group A game.

images.name: Bafana singing [1296x729]
images.caption: Bafana Bafana were in good spirits after their first point of the 2026 FIFA World Cup against Czechia in a 1-1 draw, but will likely need a win against South Korea to progress to the round of 32.
images.credit: Julian Finney - FIFA/FIFA via Getty Images

images.name: Mokoena on his emotions for his grandfather before South Africa vs. Czechia
images.caption: Mokoena on his emotions for his grandfather before South Africa vs. Czechia

articles.headline: Bosnia beat Qatar, likely to face U.S. in knockouts
articles.description: With the victory, third-place Bosnia-Herzegovina are more than likely to advance to the round of 32 in the expanded field -- and their likely opponent would be one of the tournaments co-hosts in the United States.

images.name: Bosnia celeb vs Qatar_260624 [1296x729]
images.caption: Bosnia-Herzegovina players celebrate after scoring a goal against Qatar at the World Cup.
images.alt: Bosnia-Herzegovina players celebrate after scoring a goal against Qatar at the World Cup.

images.name: Bosnia fan zone set off flares as side score opening goal vs. Qatar
images.caption: Bosnia fan zone set off flares as side score opening goal vs. Qatar.

articles.headline: Canada qualify for World Cup knockouts, miss chance to top group
articles.description: Canada lost home advantage as World Cup co-hosts after a 2-1 defeat against Switzerland denied Jesse Marsch's team top spot in Group B.

images.name: Switzerland celebrate [1296x729]
images.alt: Johan Manzambi of Switzerland celebrates 2-0  during the  World Cup match between Switzerland  v Canada at the Vancouver Stadium on June 24, 2026 in Vancouver Canada

articles.headline: World Cup Daily: Swiss beat Canada to top Group B; Messi's midnight workout
articles.description: We are now down to the final round of group stage matches at the 2026 FIFA World Cup, where all teams from the same group will be playing at the same time.

article.story:
Breel Embolo helped to dash co-hosts Canada's hopes of a last-32 clash on home soil as unbeaten Switzerland clinched top spot in Group B with a 2-1 victory in Vancouver.

The Rennes frontman set up both Rubén Vargas and Johan Manzambi after the break as the Swiss ensured they will return to the same venue in the knockout stage despite substitute Promise David's reply.

Jesse Marsch's side, who finished above Bosnia and Herzegovina by virtue of their head-to-head record, face a trip to Los Angeles.

The Swiss should have been ahead within 11 minutes when Embolo was played in behind defender Richie Laryea, only to see his shot blocked by keeper Maxime Crépeau before Manzambi's follow-up was repelled by Derek Cornelius.

Canada frontman Cyle Larin was equally wasteful two minutes later, although an offside flag belatedly spared his blushes, but he did significantly better after running on to Jonathan David's 33rd-minute pass, forcing a solid save from Gregor Kobel with a curling right-foot attempt.

Kobel clawed away Ali Ahmed's strike at his near post after he had been picked out by Jonathan David, who later lashed a left-foot shot wide, with four minutes of what proved to be a goalless first half remaining.

However, the game exploded into life within 40 seconds of the restart when Embolo smuggled the ball away from full-back Alistair Johnston on the Swiss right and crossed to the far post, where Vargas blasted past Crepeau at his near post.

The Canada keeper was culpable as Murat Yakin's men extended their lead with 57 minutes gone, allowing Manzambi's shot to squirm from his grasp after Embolo had held the ball up and laid it into his run.

Promise David gave his side hope with 14 minutes remaining when he dispatched Nathan Saliba's fine cross emphatically past Kobel, but Johnston was unable to direct his header past the keeper deep into stoppage time.
